session of her faculties, who answered all our questions readily and

for all, dispel the mystery that has so long hung ai'ound the name of
John Rutherford. He was born in the West of Ireland (Galway?) in
1829, and as a lad came with his parents to Liverpool, where her father e
was a professor of music. They were married in 1862 at the pro-cathe- .
dral in Liverpool, and two years afterwards came to London, where her
husband underwent all the difficulties incidental to a literary man,
friendless and unknown in the Metropolis. But by dint of hard and
unremitting endeavour, he slowly made his way, his first patron being
the late Frederick Greenwood, who employed him on the ‘“ Pall Mall >’

local or general history. The editor is Dr. J. S. Crone, J.P., whoi.
